meta.label: Mujadara (Lentils & Rice with Caramelized Onions)
meta.description: Classic Levantine comfort dish of lentils and rice topped with deeply caramelized onions. Naturally vegan, pantry-friendly, and excellent for meal prep.

summary: Mujadara is a classic Levantine dish made by cooking lentils and rice together and finishing with a generous layer of deeply caramelized onions. The key is patience on the onions (low/medium heat, steady stirring) to develop sweetness and a slightly crisp edge. Resting the pot after cooking helps the grains finish steaming for a fluffy texture.

timing.notes: Most active time is caramelizing onions; the rice/lentils cook mostly unattended.

diet.notes: If serving with yogurt, the dish becomes vegetarian (not vegan).

compat.notes: Best on stovetop using a heavy-bottom pot for even heat.

foodSafetyNotes: Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat until steaming hot throughout; add a splash of water if needed to loosen.

ingredients.label: Brown or green lentils
ingredients.notes: Do not use red lentils (they break down too much).

ingredients.label: Long-grain rice (basmati preferred)
ingredients.notes: Rinse until water runs mostly clear.

ingredients.label: Yellow onions, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: About 3 medium onions. Slice evenly for consistent caramelization.

ingredients.label: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use more if needed during onion caramelization.

ingredients.label: Water or vegetable stock
ingredients.notes: Approximate; adjust slightly based on pot/heat and rice brand.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt
ingredients.notes: Optional topping (makes the dish vegetarian, not vegan).

steps.title: Rinse lentils and rice
instructions: Rinse lentils in a fine strainer until the water runs mostly clear; pick out any small stones.
Rinse rice until the water runs mostly clear; drain well.

steps.title: Caramelize the onions
instructions: Heat olive oil in a wide, heavy-bottom pot over medium heat.
Add sliced onions and a pinch of the salt. Cook, stirring often, until softened and turning golden.
Reduce heat to medium-low and continue cooking 20–30 minutes, stirring regularly, until deep brown and sweet with some crisp edges.
If onions threaten to burn, lower heat and add 1–2 Tbsp water to loosen browned bits; scrape the pot and keep going.

steps.title: Reserve most onions for topping
instructions: Scoop out about 2/3 of the onions (including some of the oil) and set aside for topping.
Leave the remaining onions in the pot to flavor the rice and lentils.

steps.title: Par-cook the lentils
instructions: Add lentils to the pot with the remaining onions.
Add about 600 ml of the water/stock (reserve the rest for later) and bring to a steady simmer.
Simmer 10–12 minutes until lentils are partially tender (not fully soft).

steps.title: Add rice and spices, then simmer
instructions: Stir in rinsed rice, cumin, allspice (optional), cinnamon (optional), bay leaf (optional), remaining salt, and black pepper.
Add the remaining 300 ml water/stock (more if needed so liquid sits just above the grains by about 1/4 inch / 6 mm).
Bring back to a gentle simmer, then cover tightly and reduce heat to low.
Cook 15–18 minutes, until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.

steps.title: Rest and fluff
instructions: Turn off heat and keep covered 10 minutes to steam-finish.
Remove bay leaf (if used). Fluff gently with a fork.

steps.title: Serve with caramelized onions
instructions: Top generously with the reserved caramelized onions.
Serve with lemon wedges and chopped parsley (optional).
Optional: add a spoon of yogurt on top or serve with a cucumber-tomato salad.
